metadata.description: Personal collection of Agent Skills for agent design, evaluation, and knowledge management.

plugins.name: agent-redteam
plugins.description: Generates adversarial test cases targeting safe-failure behavior — refusal, hedging, graceful degradation. Pairs with agent-eval for scoring.

plugins.name: agent-eval
plugins.description: Designs and runs evaluations for LLM or agent outputs — rubrics, LLM-as-judge scoring, regression test sets, and pass-rate reporting.

plugins.name: deid-reid-harness
plugins.description: Adversarial de-identification vs re-identification eval harness for clinical text — generates synthetic notes with ground-truth identifier spans, a background population, diagnosis-free inference vignettes, and clinical spans, then scores three privacy attacks plus a utility axis: Safe Harbor leakage (Track 1), Expert Determination re-id risk (Track 2: k-anonymity + prosecutor/journalist/marketer), free-text inference (Track 3: recovering a withheld diagnosis, agent-eval judge path), and a privacy-utility frontier across de-id pipelines. All model-independent and verified offline with bootstrap confidence intervals and significance tests on every headline metric. People come from a pluggable source — a synthetic default or a Synthea FHIR reader for real demographics; the LLM attacker/judge is a documented swap-in.

plugins.name: repo-pincer
plugins.description: Reverse-engineers a codebase by reconciling top-down claims (docs, README, API surface) against bottom-up reality (actual implementation) — classifies every claim as Confirmed, Drift, Aspirational, or Silent. Runs standalone; optionally compiles into an Obsidian vault via wiki-operator's /source.

plugins.name: wiki-operator
plugins.description: Operates a Karpathy-style personal knowledge wiki via Obsidian MCP — searches before writing, updates existing concept pages over creating duplicates, merges redundant notes, and maintains links. Requires Obsidian MCP connected.

plugins.name: wiki-synthesizer
plugins.description: Compiles raw material into the wiki — promotes flagged ideas from journal entries into Knowledge/ concept pages and compiles Sources/raw/ files into source pages. Run after learning sessions to crystallize captured knowledge. Requires Obsidian MCP connected.

plugins.name: wiki-librarian
plugins.description: Maintains structural health of the wiki — audits for broken links, orphan pages, stale notes, near-duplicates, and contradictions. Proposes fixes with user confirmation. Run weekly or before a major synthesis session. Requires Obsidian MCP connected.

plugins.name: wiki-governor
plugins.description: Runs the wiki's self-governing maintenance loop — orchestrates wiki-librarian and wiki-synthesizer, then adds a constitution-compliance audit, a tracked health score, and a knowledge-gap queue. Run weekly to keep the vault accountable to its own rules. Requires Obsidian MCP connected.

plugins.name: wiki-warehouse
plugins.description: Ingests raw research documents (PDFs, ebooks, scans) into a separate private cold-storage GitHub repo, then writes a lean content-hash pointer note into the Obsidian vault — keeping originals and full extracted text out of the vault so it stays fast. Born-digital extraction with an OCR fallback for scans. Requires Obsidian MCP connected + the knowledge-warehouse repo cloned.
